entry_symptom: BGP peer stuck in Idle/Active/Connect state

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp summary'

nodes.question: What state is the BGP session in?

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <peer_ip>' | head -20

nodes.question: Does the BGP session show 'Idle (Admin)' or just 'Idle'?

nodes.action: Check CONFIG_DB for shutdown state: redis-cli -n 4 HGETALL 'BGP_NEIGHBOR|<peer_ip>'. If admin_status is 'down', enable with: config bgp startup neighbor <peer_ip>. Also check if bgpcfgd applied a shutdown template.
nodes.finding: BGP neighbor is administratively shut down

nodes.question: Can you ping the peer IP from the correct source interface?
Can you establish a TCP connection to the peer on port 179?

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show running-config' | grep -A5 'neighbor <peer_ip>'

nodes.question: Is the BGP configuration in CONFIG_DB consistent with FRR running config?

nodes.action: The local router sent an OPEN message but the peer did not respond. Check: (1) Peer BGP process is running, (2) AS number mismatch -- peer may be rejecting the OPEN, (3) Check peer's BGP log for notification messages. Run: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <peer_ip>' to see last notification received.

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <peer_ip>' | grep -i 'notification'

nodes.finding: Session stuck in OpenSent -- TCP connected but no OPEN response from peer

nodes.action: Both sides exchanged OPEN messages but the peer did not send a KEEPALIVE to confirm. This is rare. Check: (1) Hold timer mismatch (one side has hold=0), (2) Capability negotiation failure, (3) Peer may have crashed after sending OPEN. Check logs: docker exec bgp cat /var/log/frr/bgpd.log | tail -50
nodes.finding: Session stuck in OpenConfirm -- OPEN messages exchanged but KEEPALIVE not received

nodes.action: Verify neighbor exists in CONFIG_DB: redis-cli -n 4 KEYS 'BGP_NEIGHBOR|*'. If present but not in FRR, bgpcfgd may have failed. Check: docker exec bgp supervisorctl status bgpcfgd; journalctl -u bgp | grep bgpcfgd. If bgpcfgd is running but neighbor is missing from FRR, check for template errors in /var/log/syslog | grep bgpcfgd.

commands.command: docker exec bgp supervisorctl status bgpcfgd

nodes.finding: BGP neighbor not configured or bgpcfgd has not synced config to FRR

nodes.action: Troubleshoot IP connectivity: (1) Check interface status: show interfaces status, (2) Check IP addresses: show ip interface, (3) Check routing to peer: show ip route <peer_ip>, (4) Check ARP: show arp | grep <peer_ip>, (5) If peer is multi-hop, check intermediate path with traceroute.
nodes.finding: Layer 3 connectivity to peer is broken

nodes.action: Check: (1) ACL/iptables blocking port 179: iptables -L -n | grep 179, (2) CoPP policy dropping BGP: check copp configuration, (3) Peer's BGP process not running, (4) Peer is listening on a different IP (update-source mismatch). Also verify SONiC CoPP is not rate-limiting BGP: redis-cli -n 4 HGETALL 'COPP_TABLE|copp-system-bgp'.
nodes.finding: TCP port 179 blocked or peer BGP not listening

nodes.action: This is a bgpcfgd issue. Check bgpcfgd logs: journalctl -u bgp | grep bgpcfgd. Common causes: (1) bgpcfgd crashed or stuck, (2) Jinja2 template rendering error, (3) Missing required fields in CONFIG_DB BGP_NEIGHBOR entry. Try restarting bgpcfgd: docker exec bgp supervisorctl restart bgpcfgd. If that fails, check the template at /usr/share/sonic/templates/bgpd.conf.j2.

commands.command: docker exec bgp supervisorctl status bgpcfgd

nodes.finding: bgpcfgd has not synced CONFIG_DB neighbor to FRR

nodes.action: Verify AS numbers match: (1) CONFIG_DB: redis-cli -n 4 HGETALL 'BGP_NEIGHBOR|<peer_ip>' -- check 'asn' field, (2) FRR: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <peer_ip>' -- check 'remote AS'. If they differ, the peer will reject the OPEN message with a NOTIFICATION (Bad Peer AS). Fix in CONFIG_DB: config bgp neighbor <peer_ip> remote-as <correct_asn>.

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <peer_ip>' | grep 'remote AS'

nodes.finding: AS number mismatch between CONFIG_DB and FRR or between peers

nodes.action: Advanced debugging: (1) Check for authentication mismatch: redis-cli -n 4 HGETALL 'BGP_NEIGHBOR|<peer_ip>' | grep password, (2) Check for capability mismatch in NOTIFICATION: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <peer_ip>' | grep -i 'capability', (3) Check for maximum-prefix limit exceeded, (4) Packet capture: tcpdump -i <interface> -n 'tcp port 179' -c 20. (5) Check if this is a human error -- someone configured via vtysh directly.

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <peer_ip>'
sudo tcpdump -i any -n 'tcp port 179 and host <peer_ip>' -c 10

nodes.finding: Configuration appears correct but session still not establishing
